meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use SaaS pricing strategies to overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. An advanced checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid delivery.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a perception issue, not a skill issue. By implementing a high-value SaaS pricing model—specifically value-based pricing—you pivot from 'contractor' to 'business partner.' Use Antigravity to scaffold the architecture and Claude Code to automate the implementation, proving your expertise through tangible, high-quality deliverables.
content.intro:
The cold sweat of a blank portfolio is the rite of passage for every elite freelancer. You have the technical chops, but the market demands social proof you haven't yet manufactured. While others scramble for low-paying gigs to fill their resumes, you are going to bypass the noise by engineering a high-value SaaS solution that speaks for itself.

We aren't building just another clone; we are architecting a pricing-first SaaS product that demonstrates your mastery of complex systems. By leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code, you will collapse months of development into days, turning your 'lack of portfolio' into a showcase of sophisticated, production-ready infrastructure.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Stop charging by the hour. Map out three tiers (Basic, Pro, Enterprise) that solve specific pain points for a niche client base. This establishes you as a strategist.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SaaS pricing schema in JSON format that supports tiered subscription logic, usage-based metering, and feature-flagging for a B2B productivity tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the database schema for the pricing tiers defined in the project docs.'

steps.title: Architect the Metering Engine
steps.description: Your pricing model is only as good as your billing logic. Use Antigravity to build a robust, error-free usage tracker.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust Node.js middleware to track API usage per user and calculate real-time overage costs based on the tiered pricing model.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement the usage-tracking middleware and integrate it with the existing Stripe API setup.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration
steps.description: Secure, professional payments are the bedrock of trust. Connect your architecture to Stripe to handle subscription life cycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Stripe webhook handler to manage subscription renewals, cancellations, and trial period logic for our SaaS platform.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install stripe && claude run "Integrate Stripe webhooks to update user subscription status in the database."'

steps.title: Build the Client Dashboard
steps.description: Create a high-fidelity dashboard that shows the user their current usage and upgrade options, proving your UI/UX competence.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React dashboard component that visualizes real-time usage metrics against the user's current pricing plan limits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the UsageDashboard component with charts showing current usage versus plan limits.'

steps.title: Automate Documentation & Deployment
steps.description: Professional output requires professional documentation. Use Claude Code to generate your README and CI/CD pipelines.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional technical README that explains the pricing architecture and setup process for potential clients.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a comprehensive README.md and a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment.'

pro_tips: Always include a 'Custom/Enterprise' tier that requires a manual quote; it forces a conversation and positions you as a consultant.
Use 'Price Anchoring' in your UI by highlighting the middle tier as the 'Most Popular' to increase conversion rates.
Ship your SaaS as an open-source project first; it builds massive authority and functions as a living portfolio piece.
Integrate a 'Usage Alert' system that emails users before they hit limits—this is the kind of detail that makes clients pay a premium.
Document your architecture decisions in the repo; clients want to see *how* you think, not just what you built.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to a staging environment, record a 60-second Loom walkthrough of the pricing architecture, and include that link in your outreach emails to prospects.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to build a professional-grade SaaS that validates your skills and justifies premium pricing. Don't let the 'no portfolio' excuse hold you back when you have the tools to ship in record time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
